content: Dexie.js versioning pitfall: once a db version N is deployed to production, rolling back code to version N-1 will cause db.open() to fail because user's browser already has version N installed — Dexie throws VersionError. Mitigation: never decrement version numbers; add a cleanup/migration-only version bump instead. Transaction scope: Dexie transactions auto-close after the last awaited operation in the callback; storing a reference outside the transaction and using it later causes 'Transaction is not active' errors. Upgrade atomicity: if any upgrader function throws, the entire upgrade transaction rolls back, leaving the db unopened until the error is fixed. Storage limits: Chrome/Firefox use Storage API best-effort mode by default; entire origin data (IndexedDB + Cache API + localStorage) is evicted together under storage pressure, not trimmed per-table.
